item.title: test notebook
item.description:
Notes

  • Test (184) to see if SoC makes a difference. Make a decision: SoC or not.
  • Check that I have selected right options for Born effective charge / dielectric constant if needed.
  • Get free IR spectra to see if working. If so, NWRITE=3 for CdTe.
  • Can use CHGCAR as starting point in phonons?
  • tetrahedron method is supposed to converge faster than the others

Commands

  • ATOM_TYPE is important and must correspond to the numbers on sixth line of the POSCAR
  • PRIMITIVE_AXIS needs to be used to map supercells built from conventional cells onto primitive cell band structures
  • Create the displaced structures for a defective cell: phonopy -d --dim='1 1 1' --tolerance=1e-4
  • If using the tolerance tag this must continue to be used for the FORCE_SETS generation and any subsequent band/dos calculations
  • Create FORCE_SETS: phonopy -f disp/{001...008}/vasprun.xml --tolerance=1e-3
  • the disp.yaml will need to be in current folder
  • plot band: phonopy -s -p band.conf --tolerance=1e-3
  • the POSCAR will have to be in the current folder
  • plot dos/thermal: phonopy -s -p (-t) dos.conf

For Perfect CdTe (444 supercell)

  • Tested for convergence of thermal props and DOS on 48 48 48 and 60 60 60 k-mesh

Animation

Thanks to Adam, it’s super easy to generate lovely .gif files of the phonon modes

1) Generate .ascii file in phonopy (choosing the phonon q):

phonopy anime.conf

2) Call ascii-phonons to do it’s magic:

~/Applications/ascii-phonons/scripts/ascii-phonons anime.ascii -m 0 --gif -d 4 4 4  -o pretty0

The first and second lowest phonon mode at the X point.

The gamma modes in increasing frequency (first 3 are acoustic, after that they are optic).

For perfect GaAs (444 supercell)

Results when forces were calculated with SoC

CdTe neutral Te defect

  • Not happy with the soft modes: is this becaase neutral defect not energetically stable?
  • Need to band unfold

MAPI perfect

  • Not happy with soft modes at gamma
  • code it be because of neglection of spin in the initial relaxation?
  • Checked forces on supercell, but will not relax explicitly to see if it makes any difference: I am also going to use ISPIN=2 all the way through

CsSnI3 perfect

  • happy with this: agrees perfectly with Lora’s results
